brawling_blademaster.name: Brawling Blademaster
description: ## Brawling Blademaster
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/10›

While some martial arts traditions focus on the strength of the body, most schools of combat and self-defense know the importance of using weapons as well. Brawling blademaster samurai use weapon strikes and unarmed attacks in equal measure - foes expecting to defend themselves against a dagger or a sword are often caught off guard by a brawling blademaster's brutal kicks or punches.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="Brawling blademasters are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/katana› and ‹eq-weapon/wakizashi›. Brawling blademasters are proficient with light armor, but not with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Empty Hand (Ex)]{icon=melee ability="A brawling blademaster gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. She uses her samurai level as her ‹class/monk› level for determining the amount of damage she deals with an unarmed strike. These levels stack with monk levels to determine the brawling blademaster's unarmed strike damage." replace=Mount}
::ab[Nimble (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A brawling blademaster gains the ‹class/gunslinger«'s› nimble class feature, using her samurai level as his gunslinger level." head}

::ab[Nimble (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the gunslinger ability." next icon=def passive="You gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC while wearing light or no armor. Anything that causes you to lose your Dexterity bonus to AC also causes you to lose this dodge bonus." increment="The dodge bonus~2~4"}

::ab[Dual Expertise (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A brawling blademaster gains ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites. Furthermore, when the brawling blademaster selects a feat with the *bonus feat* class feature, she can ignore any Dexterity requirements for the feat if its prerequisites include the Two-Weapon Fighting feat." replace="Weapon expertise"}

::ab[Harmonious Flow (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="The brawling blademaster becomes skilled at combining weapon attacks and unarmed strikes into a harmonious combat style." ability="When attacking the target of her *challenge,* the brawling blademaster doesn't take the usual -2 penalty on attack rolls associated with attacking with two weapons if her main-hand weapon is a light or one-handed manufactured weapon and her off-hand weapon is an unarmed strike (or vice versa)." special="While she's using this ability, one of her hands must be empty even if she uses another body part to make an unarmed strike." replace="Mounted archer"}

::ab[Perfect Clarity (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def flavor="The brawling blademaster sees all her foes with perfect clarity even when focused on one opponent." passive="She no longer takes a penalty to her AC during a *challenge.*" replace=Banner}
::ab[Superior Focus (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def passive="The brawling blademaster gains a +2 bonus on saving throws during a *challenge.*" replace="Greater banner"}

ironbound_sword.name: Ironbound Sword
description: ## Ironbound Sword
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/20›

The ironbound sword's fighting style, focused on incapacitating opponents rather than killing them, is founded on a philosophy that cares both for the samurai and for her attacker. This style became prevalent during the peaceful and stable periods of Lung Wa, and reflected those values. Using these techniques, an ironbound sword can subdue a foe without causing permanent harm, leaving the soul of the samurai unstained by deaths of her enemies.

::ab[Merciful Combatant (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="An ironbound sword becomes an expert at defeating foes without killing them." passive="An ironbound sword can use any weapon to deal nonlethal damage without taking the normal -4 penalty on attack rolls. Additionally, the ironbound sword gains a +2 bonus on combat maneuvers against a target so long as the last successful attack she made against that target dealt nonlethal damage." special="Her samurai levels count as fighter levels and stack with fighter levels for the purposes of fighter and samurai prerequisites and class features." replace="Weapon expertise"}

::ab[Subduing Knockout (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-lower ability="Once per day, an ironbound sword can unleash a devastating attack that can instantly knock a target unconscious. She must announce this intent before making her attack roll. If the ironbound sword's attack hits and the target takes damage from the blow, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the ironbound sword's level + the higher of the ironbound sword's Strength or Dexterity modifier) or fall unconscious for 1d6 rounds. Each round on its turn, the unconscious target can attempt a new saving throw to end the effect as a full-round action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Creatures immune to critical hits or nonlethal damage are immune to this ability." imp10="The ironbound sword can use this ability twice per day." imp15="She can use this three times per day." replace=Banner}

::ab[Blade Reversal (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def flavor="An ironbound sword can turn the force of an opponent's attacks back against them." order=af ability="Whenever the ironbound sword takes a ‹misc/full defense› action, she can expend an attack of opportunity to attempt to counter melee attacks made against her. This counter is a single attack roll at the ironbound blade's full base attack bonus that resolves before the enemy's attack. If the counter successfully hits the attacking enemy's AC and if the ironbound sword's attack roll is higher than the attacking creature's attack roll, the ironbound sword takes no damage from the enemy's attack and can initiate a ‹rule/disarm› combat maneuver against the attacking enemy as a free action. If the triggering attack was made with a natural attack or unarmed strike, a successful disarm combat maneuver renders that natural attack or unarmed strike unusable until the victim spends a standard action to reset dislocated joints." free="In addition, the ironbound sword can spend one use of her *resolve* when she hits with a nonlethal attack to initiate a disarm combat maneuver against her target." replace="Greater banner"}

sovereign_blade.name: Sovereign Blade
description: ## Sovereign Blade
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/14›
In Tian Xia, many samurai are tutored in the ways of honor using lessons that originate from sovereign dragons and their dedication to safeguarding harmony.

::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning note="A sovereign blade's alignment must be at least partially neutral. If the sovereign blade's alignment is not partially neutral, she is treated as if she had violated her order's edicts each day until her alignment is again partially neutral."}

::ab[Dogmatic Denial (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A sovereign blade gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against spells with any of the following descriptors that don't match part of the character's own alignment: chaotic, evil, good, or lawful." replace=Mount}

::ab[Golden Armor (Su)]{l=4 icon=def ability="A sovereign blade can spend one use of her *resolve* to cause her armor to become golden for 1 round per class level, gaining energy resistance equal to 1/2 her samurai level against one energy type of her choice." replace="Mounted archer"}

::ab[Sovereign Breath (Sp)]{l=6 icon=cone ability="The sovereign blade can issue forth an immense roar similar to the breath weapon of a ‹family/sovereign dragon›. This functions as ‹spell/dragon's breath›, except it deals sonic damage in a 30-foot cone and has a caster level equal to half the sovereign blade's samurai level." useInc=samurai~6 useF="6~1" replace="Bonus feats gained at 6th, 12th, and 18th level"}

sword_saint.name: Sword Saint
description: ## Sword Saint
‹SOURCE Dragon Empires Primer/22›

Sword saints hail from lands where samurai are prevalent, and are often ‹order/ronin› who wander the world seeking new challenges to perfect their intricate style of swordplay called iaijutsu. The following benefits apply only when a sword saint is using a sword and carrying nothing in his other hand.

::ab[Iaijutsu Strike (Ex)]{icon=melee-down order=an flavor="A sword saint can perform a lightning-quick *iaijutsu strike* against the target of his *challenge* to inflict devastating wounds while drawing his sword." ability="After the sword saint has *challenged* a foe but before he has attacked the target of his *challenge,* he may choose to use his *iaijutsu strike* as a @HLfull-round_action, making an attack roll with his weapon as normal. In order to use this ability, the sword saint's weapon must be sheathed at the start of his turn. If he successfully hits his opponent with an *iaijutsu strike,* his attack deals an additional +1d6 points of damage." note="After making an *iaijutsu strike,* a sword saint takes a -4 penalty to his AC until his next turn, but his weapon is now drawn and he may continually to fight normally. Regardless of whether he hits his opponent with the *iaijutsu strike,* a sword saint cannot use this ability on the same foe more than once per day." incrementPlain="The bonus damage~3~2" incrementEnd="d6." special="Any extra damage as a result of a successful *iaijutsu strike* is not multiplied by a critical hit." imp10="A sword saint learns to focus faster and is able to make an *iaijutsu strike* as a @HLstandard_action, and the penalty to his AC is reduced to -2." replace=Mount}

::ab[Brutal Slash (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="A sword saint's *iaijutsu strike* becomes even more deadly." ability="If a sword saint threatens a critical hit with his *iaijutsu strike,* he adds a bonus equal to 1/2 his class level to the attack roll to confirm a critical hit." replace="Mounted charge"}

::ab[Terrifying Iaijutsu (Ex)]{l=5 icon=lower flavor="A sword saint's *iaijutsu strike* devastates the morale of foes that witness it." ability="When a sword saint successfully hits with an *iaijutsu strike,* all foes within 30 feet must succeed at a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the sword saint's class level + the sword saint's Cha modifier) or become ‹misc/shaken› for 1d4+1 rounds." replace=Banner}

::ab[Roaring Iaijutsu (Ex)]{l=14 icon=lower flavor="A sword saint's *iaijutsu strike* deafens foes upon impact." ability="When a sword saint successfully hits with an *iaijutsu strike,* all foes within 30 feet must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 the sword saint's class level + the sword saint's Str modifier) or be ‹misc/deafened› for 1d4 minutes." replace="Greater banner"}

ward_speaker.name: Ward Speaker
description: ## Ward Speaker
‹SOURCE Disciple's Doctrine/26›
The ward speaker draws power from simple rituals to honor the spirits found throughout the world so that he might better protect those entrusted to his care.
::prereq{other="Nonevil alignment"}

::ab[Propitiation (Ex, Su, Sp)]{icon=power-roll head order=uai ability="A ward speaker can perform a special ritual to *propitiate* ‹family/kami› and gain a sliver of their power. This ritual must be performed at a shrine or a location warded by a specific type of kami. The *propitiation* takes 1 minute and involves clapping, ringing bells, bowing, and token offerings of coins, food, incense, or other gifts. The ward speaker's *propitiation* must be performed uninterrupted to receive any benefits from it. At the end of this ritual, the ward speaker immediately gains a *fortune* depending on the type of kami he sought to *propitiate.*" immediate="The ward speaker can dismiss the *fortune* to reroll one d20 roll before the results of the roll are revealed; he must use the result of the second roll, even if it's worse." special="If not dismissed, a *fortune* remains in effect until the ward speaker regains the use of his daily abilities, at which point the *fortune* and its benefits end. If a ward speaker dismisses a *fortune* to receive a benefit based on the specific kami he *propitiated* (see below), he does not also gain the reroll described here. He can maintain only one *fortune* at a time; performing a *propitiation* while he already has a *fortune* active ends the previous *fortune* and its benefits." useInc="samurai~3~1" useF="1~1~3" replace="Resolve~greater resolve~true resolve"}

::ab[Jinushigami (Ex, Sp)]{next icon=boost passive="The ward speaker (and his mount, if he has one) gains a 10-foot enhancement bonus to base speed." imp9="This enhancement bonus increases to 20 feet." imp17="Once per day, he can dismiss this *fortune* as an @HLimmediate_action to cast ‹spell/earthquake› as a spell-like ability, using his samurai level as his caster level."}

::ab[Kodama (Su)]{next icon=lower standard="The ward speaker can afflict one target that can see him with a distracting ‹umr/gaze›. If the target fails a Will save (DC = 10 + half the ward speaker's samurai level), it is ‹misc/staggered› for 1d4 rounds." imp9="It is instead staggered for 2d4 rounds." imp17="It is instead staggered for 1 minute." special="Once a creature has been targeted by this ability, it cannot be targeted by it again for 24 hours"}

::ab[Shikigami (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="The ward speaker gains ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› and ‹feat/Throw Anything› as bonus feats." imp9="He gains ‹feat/Toughness› and ‹feat/Step Up›." imp17="He gains ‹feat/Following Step› and ‹feat/Step Up and Strike›, even if he does not meet the prerequisites."}

::ab[Toshigami (Sp)]{next icon=magic ability="The ward speaker can speak with plants, as the ‹spell/speak with plants› spell." imp9="The ward speaker can dismiss this *fortune* as an @HLimmediate_action to cast ‹spell/haste› as a spell-like ability." imp17="Once per day, the ward speaker can instead dismiss this *fortune* to cast ‹spell/temporal stasis› as a spell-like ability." special="The ward speaker's caster level for these spell-like abilities is equal to his samurai level."}

::ab[Zuishin (Sp)]{next icon=aid immediate="The ward speaker can dismiss this *fortune* to cast ‹spell/cure light wounds› as a spell-like ability." imp9="The ward speaker can dismiss this *fortune* to cast ‹spell/cure serious wounds›, ‹spell/lesser restoration›, ‹spell/remove curse›, or ‹spell/remove disease›." imp17="He also adds ‹spell/breath of life› and ‹spell/heal› to this list." special="The ward speaker's caster level for these spell-like abilities is equal to his samurai level."}

::ab[Resilient Stand (Ex)]{l=11 icon=roll flavor="The spirits can bolster a ward speaker's willpower and resolve, granting him an inner reservoir he can use when he needs it most." ability="If a ward speaker has not expended his daily uses of *propitiation,* he can call upon the spirits to reroll a saving throw during an *honorable stand* instead of expending a use of *resolve* to do so. He must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse. This expends a daily use of *propitiation.*" alter="Honorable stand"}

warrior_poet.name: Warrior Poet
description: ## Warrior Poet
‹SOURCE Heroes from the Fringe/13›

Warrior poets often study calligraphy, flower arrangement, poetry, and other courtly arts, but when called to battle, they treat combat as its own art form, fighting with beauty and grace. While these traditions first arose among the Jininese elves and are still extremely common in Jinin, the warrior poet's art has spread to different nations and peoples in Tian Xia over centuries.

::ab[Dancer's Grace (Ex)]{icon=def passive="When wearing no armor and not using a shield, the warrior poet gains a bonus to Armor Class equal to her Charisma bonus (to a maximum of her samurai level). A warrior poet loses this bonus while ‹rule/flat-footed› or otherwise denied her Dexterity bonus." replace="Proficiency with medium armor, heavy armor, and shields"}

::ab[Flourish (Ex)]{head icon=power flavor="The warrior poet is skilled at performing elegant moves in battle." l1="The warrior poet gains a flourish of her choice from the list below." repeatAt="She gains another flourish.~3~5~9~13~17~20" replace="Mount~weapon expertise~banner~greater banner"}

::ab[Chrysanthemum's Blooming]{next icon=power prereq="11th-level samurai" ability="The warrior poet gains ‹feat/Vital Strike› as a bonus feat and can apply its benefit when using ‹feat/Spring Attack›." imp16="If the warrior poet has ‹feat/Improved Vital Strike›, she can apply that feat's benefit instead."}

::ab[Exodus of Jinin]{next icon=boost passive="As long as the warrior poet is wearing light or no armor and carrying no more than a light load, her land speed increases by 10 feet." special="A warrior poet can select this flourish up to three times."}

::ab[Harmony of the Tranquil Garden]{next icon=power move="The warrior poet can focus her senses to gain ‹umr/blindsense› out to 5 feet for 1 round." incrementPlain="The range of her blindsense~5~5~10~5" incrementEnd=" feet."}

::ab[Jininsiel's Guidance]{next icon=power prereq="4th-level samuari" ability="The warrior poet gains the rogue's ‹ability/uncanny dodge› class feature." special="This flourish can be selected a second time beginning at 8th level; the warrior poet then gains the rogue's ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› class feature."}

::ab[Kitsune's Mystique]{next icon=power ability="The warrior poet gains ‹feat/Improved Feint› as a bonus feat, ignoring its prerequisites. When the warrior poet uses Spring Attack or takes a move action to move, she can attempt to feint against one creature she threatens during her movement as part of her movement."}

::ab[Petals on the Wind]{next icon=power ability="Whenever a foe provokes an attack of opportunity from the warrior poet, she can move 5 feet before making the attack of opportunity. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. If the warrior poet takes an action to move during her next turn, she subtracts 5 feet from her total movement for each time she has used this ability since her last turn."}
::ab[Wrath of the Heavens]{next icon=power ability="The warrior poet gains ‹feat/Shot on the Run› as a bonus feat, ignoring its prerequisites."}

::ab[Graceful Warrior (Ex)]{icon=power ability="The warrior poet gains ‹feat/Weapon Finesse› as a bonus feat and can apply its benefits to ‹eq-weapon/glaive«s›, ‹eq-weapon/katana«s›, and ‹eq-weapon/naginata«s› as if they were light weapons. This does not alter the weapons' properties for the purposes of any other effects."}

::ab[Skirmisher's Challenge (Ex)]{icon=down ability="The warrior poet adds her samurai level to her damage rolls only on her first successful attack against a *challenged* target each round." alter=Challenge}

::ab[Graceful Strike (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="When the warrior poet uses Weapon Finesse to make a melee attack that adds her Dexterity bonus to attack rolls and her Strength bonus to damage rolls, she also adds half her samurai level to damage rolls." replace="Mounted archer~demanding challenge"}

::ab[Battle Dance (Ex)]{icon=power l6="The warrior poet gains Spring Attack as a bonus feat." l12="She gains ‹feat/Improved Spring Attack› as a bonus feat." l18="She gains ‹feat/Greater Spring Attack› as a bonus feat." special="The warrior poet does not need to meet the prerequisites for these feats." replace="Bonus combat feats"}

yojimbo.name: Yojimbo
description: ## Yojimbo
‹SOURCE Armor Master's Handbook/7›

Yojimbo are highly trained bodyguards favored by the nobles and warlords of distant Minkai. Yojimbo who follow the order of the warrior loyally guard a single master, while ronin serve whoever can pay for their protection. Yojimbo often accompany visitors to the Inner Sea region from Tian Xia; a few have chosen to stay, and some have even passed on their training to bodyguards of this region.

::ab[Resolute Defense (Ex)]{l=1 icon=protect-boost ability="Whenever a yojimbo issues a challenge, he can select one ally as his ward for the duration of the challenge. Whenever the yojimbo is adjacent to his ward, he can use *resolve, greater resolve,* and *true resolve* on his ward rather than targeting himself." replace=Mount}

::ab[Armor Expertise (Ex)]{l=3 head icon=def ability="A yojimbo selects one type of armor, such as ‹eq-armor/chain shirt› or ‹eq-armor/scale mail›. The yojimbo gains *armor training,* treating his samurai level as his ‹class/fighter› level, when wearing the selected armor." replace="Weapon expertise"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="Text from the fighter class ability." l3="Whenever you wear armor, you reduce the armor check penalty by 1 (to a minimum of 0) and increase the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by the armor by 1. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing medium armor." l7="The penalty is now reduced by 2 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +2. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing heavy armor." l11="The penalty is now reduced by 3 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +3." l15="The penalty is now reduced by 4 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +4."}

::ab[Intercept (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-protect flavor="A yojimbo learns to better disrupt the attacks of his enemies." ability="He gains ‹feat/Bodyguard› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisite. In addition, whenever the yojimbo uses the ‹rule/aid another<_2› action to increase an ally's Armor Class, the bonus to Armor Class from the aid another action increases by 1." replace="Mounted archer"}
